creator: michael.hmich@gmail.com
text:
I see, no problem

this partition table seems not to cause any harm for the three different linux installed

what I found is that there is a msdos/mbr table at the beginning and a gpd backup at the end of the disk, that must be the reason for the part number 5, it is on the gpt table and not visible for Freebsd

on the other hand I can mount two from linux partitions under freebsd from cmd line

I think that I have to solve my linux table mismatch by backing up and create a new clean partition table

#################

now what concerns to FreebSD

freebsd reject linux partitions configured in /etc/fstab while the same partitions can be mounted with the same options from the mount cmd line 

fstab
/dev/ada0s4  /mnt   ext2fs rw

mount mnt failes, no matter if it is ada0s4 or ada0p4
the error displayed is no such file or device

cmd line
mount /dev/ada0p4 -t ext2fs /mnt 

works


should I open a new report or let this here going as is?
